library.title: Library
library.description: An encyclopedia of planets, stars, and constellations is on its way.

faqs.q: How do I navigate the 3D explorer?
faqs.a: Use your mouse to orbit (left-click drag), zoom (scroll wheel), and pan (right-click drag). On touch devices, use two fingers to zoom and one finger to rotate.

faqs.q: How do I change the scale view?
faqs.a: Use the scale selector in the top bar to switch between Solar System, Stellar, Galactic, and Cosmic scales. You can also zoom out naturally to trigger scale transitions.

faqs.q: How do I save a bookmark?
faqs.a: Press Ctrl+B (or Cmd+B on Mac) at any time while exploring, or click the bookmark icon in the top bar. Give your view a name and save it. Bookmarks restore your exact camera position and scale.

faqs.q: How do I view my achievements?
faqs.a: Go to Profile from the sidebar and select the Achievements tab. You can also see newly unlocked achievements as toast notifications while exploring.

faqs.q: How do I change simulation speed?
faqs.a: Use the speed control in the explorer toolbar. You can speed up, slow down, pause, or reset the simulation. Time-traveling far into the future or past also earns XP.

faqs.q: What is the difference between High Quality and Low Quality mode?
faqs.a: High Quality renders full particle counts and high resolution — best for powerful devices. Low Quality reduces particle counts and caps resolution for smoother performance on low-end hardware. Change this in Settings.

welcome.title: Welcome to Cosmic Explorer
welcome.description: An interactive journey through our solar system and beyond. Let's explore the universe together.

sun.title: The Sun
sun.description: Our star at the center of the solar system, containing 99.8% of all the mass in the solar system.

earth.title: Earth
earth.description: Our home planet — the only known world harboring life, with liquid water oceans and a protective atmosphere.

saturn.title: Saturn
saturn.description: The jewel of the solar system, famous for its spectacular ring system made of ice and rock particles.

neptune.title: Neptune
neptune.description: The farthest planet from the Sun, 30 times Earth's distance away, with winds reaching 2,100 km/h.

stellar.title: Stellar Neighborhood
stellar.description: Zoom out beyond our solar system. Thousands of nearby stars become visible within 100 light-years.

galactic.title: The Milky Way
galactic.description: Our home galaxy — a spiral galaxy containing 200 to 400 billion stars, 100,000 light-years across.

cosmic.title: The Observable Universe
cosmic.description: The largest scale — the entire observable universe, 93 billion light-years in diameter, containing trillions of galaxies.

finish.title: You're Ready to Explore!
finish.description: You've seen the full scale of the cosmos. Now explore freely — click planets, zoom in and out, discover the universe.
